title: Callitriche occidentalis
taxonomy: Callitriche occidentalis is a species of aquatic plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a type of water starwort that is commonly found in shallow water environments. The plant is native to Western North America and is often associated with slow-moving streams and rivers. As a member of the family Plantaginaceae, it is a flowering plant that belongs to the order Lamiales. The classification of Callitriche occidentalis places it within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Callitriche occidentalis is not well-documented, however, it is used in some folk remedies. It's said to have uses in treating fever and various ailments. The plant is used in a decoction, where it's steeped in water to make a medicinal tea. However, its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven. More research is needed to confirm its medicinal properties and uses.
